steps.title: Find the slope of line h from the table
steps.content: Pick any two ordered pairs from the table and apply the slope formula $m=\frac{y_2-y_1}{x_2-x_1}$. The table values yield a constant slope of $m=6$.

steps.title: Write the equation of line h
steps.content:
Use point-slope form $y-y_1=m(x-x_1)$ with any table point and $m=6$, then solve for $y$. The line simplifies to:
$$y = 6x + 22$$

steps.title: Translate down 5 units and solve for the x-intercept of line k
steps.content:
Translating a line down 5 units subtracts 5 from the constant term:
$$y = 6x + 22 - 5 = 6x + 17$$
Set $y=0$ to find the $x$-intercept:
$$0 = 6x + 17 \;\Rightarrow\; x = -\frac{17}{6}$$
So the $x$-intercept of line $k$ is $\left(-\frac{17}{6},0\right)$ — D is correct.
